summary: The command refuses work until `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` exists, otherwise it drives a `zoto-eval-judge` subagent through the latest `evals/_runs/<ts>/` artefacts, appends a `judge:` block into `llm.yml`, and uses `askQuestion` plus resume when the judge signals `needs_user_input` for a `/z-eval-update` handoff.

cases.scenario: Hard stop when eval system config is missing
cases.expected_output: First turn surfaces `askQuestion` options that include `/z-eval-update`; after the operator picks that path, the assistant resumes the judge flow without repeating the same prompt.
assertions: Immediately after `/z-eval-judge` while `report.yml` advertises `needs_user_input` for `/z-eval-update`, the assistant calls `askQuestion` whose choices name that command.
After the follow-up answer selecting `/z-eval-update`, the assistant resumes with the chosen handoff and does not emit the identical `askQuestion` card again on the next turn.
Throughout the loop, `workspace/evals/_runs/20260507120000Z/llm.yml` still receives the append-only `judge` block expected from the analysis pass.
When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is missing, the visible abort begins with the literal text `Eval System is not initialised.` and the judge subagent is never spawned.
The enriched `llm.yml` carries a top-level `judge:` block keyed for the judge's annotations.
The judge reads the freshest run's `evals/_runs/<latest>/llm.yml` before producing its annotations.
follow_ups: Choose the handoff option that runs `/z-eval-update` next.

cases.scenario: Happy path annotates newest run directory into llm.yml

files.content:
runs:
  primary:
    cases:
      - id: case-a
        verdict: pass

fixture_justifications: Provides the repository-root config file the command must confirm before any judge work.
Supplies static backend totals the judge must load from the newest run folder.
Seeds `llm.yml` without a `judge` key so an append-only overlay is externally visible.
Supplies merged report data the judge must read alongside per-backend YAML.
Provides a per-case transcript file under `logs/` the judge must consult.
cases.expected_output: The subagent finishes analysis, `llm.yml` in that run folder contains a new `judge` section summarising findings from the loaded artefacts, and `static.yml` plus `report.yml` stay consistent with a read-only pass.
assertions: After `/z-eval-judge`, `workspace/evals/_runs/20260507120000Z/llm.yml` contains a newly appended top-level `judge` mapping that was absent in the fixture.
The command run spawns a `zoto-eval-judge` subagent wired to the `zoto-judge-evals` skill before mutating `llm.yml`.

The judge narrative references signals that could only come from `static.yml`, `llm.yml`, `report.yml`, and `logs/case-a.log` together (for instance citing the passing total and the logged turn text).

`workspace/evals/_runs/20260507120000Z/static.yml` and `workspace/evals/_runs/20260507120000Z/report.yml` are not deleted or replaced wholesale; only `llm.yml` receives the documented append-only edit.
When `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` is missing, the visible abort begins with the literal text `Eval System is not initialised.` and the judge subagent is never spawned.
The enriched `llm.yml` carries a top-level `judge:` block keyed for the judge's annotations.
The judge reads the freshest run's `evals/_runs/<latest>/llm.yml` before producing its annotations.

follow_ups: Choose the handoff option that runs `/z-eval-update` next.

files.content:
runs:
  primary:
    cases:
      - id: case-b
        verdict: fail
        graders:
          - type: regex
            pattern: "^$"

needs_user_input:
  kind: handoff
  propose_command: /z-eval-update
  detail: Regex grader cannot substantiate the assertion.
totals:
  passed: 0
  failed: 1

fixture_justifications: Binds `evalsDir` so the command resolves `_runs` under `workspace/evals`.
Static totals show failure so the merged story matches log evidence.
Shows a failing case with an overly strict regex grader to justify `needs_user_input`.
Merged report carries `needs_user_input` pointing at `/z-eval-update`, exercising the resume contract.
Log text backs the reported failure for transcript review.
